channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Novo Nordisk Files Lawsuit Against Eli Lilly Over Weight-Loss Ad Claims
item.description: Novo Nordisk has filed a lawsuit against Eli Lilly in a U.S. federal court, alleging that Lilly's advertising for its weight-loss drugs is deceptive. The Danish pharmaceutical company claims that Lill...

item.title: Inmates Graduate from Yale Prison Program, Highlighting Educational Reform
item.description: Seven inmates at a Connecticut prison have become the first graduates of a joint Yale and University of New Haven education program, marking a historic milestone in correctional education. Launched in...

item.title: FDA Continues Cyclospora Investigation on Taylor Farms Lettuce Despite False Positive Test
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) confirmed that a previous false positive test result for cyclospora in Taylor Farms lettuce does not alter its ongoing investigation into the outbreak. Desp...

item.title: Nigerian lawmakers push for action on food safety to curb preventable deaths
item.description: Nigerian lawmakers have raised alarm over the alarming number of annual deaths caused by unsafe food, calling for urgent government intervention. The motion highlights that Nigeria records approximate...

item.title: Reevaluating AI's Impact on Employment: China's Role vs. Sinophobic Policies
item.description: The article explores the growing debate around artificial intelligence (AI) and its impact on employment, challenging the common narrative that AI will destroy jobs or be dominated by a single expensi...

item.title: Donald Kaberuka's impact on African economic development
item.description: Donald Kaberuka, a Rwandan refugee who fled persecution in 1959, has become a key figure in shaping Africa's economy over three decades. After earning a Master's in development studies from the Univer...

item.title: Top 2026 World Cup Players By Instagram Follower Growth
item.description: Erling Haaland, the Norway striker, topped the Instagram follower count for the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a massive 32.1 million new followers. His seven goals and joint third place in the Golden Boot ...

item.title: Sean McCann to Compete for Scotland in Commonwealth Games
item.description: Sean McCann, the younger brother of Madeleine McCann, has been selected to compete for Scotland at the upcoming Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. The 21-year-old swimmer will participate in freestyle eve...

item.title: Donald Trump's Controversial Role in Sports Governance
item.description: The article discusses the growing politicization of sports, highlighting Donald Trump's controversial interventions in major events. It focuses on Bryson DeChambeau's alleged threat to call Trump over...

item.title: Nairobi Lawyer Arrested in Land Fraud Case
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has arrested Nairobi-based lawyer Abdulahi Musdaf Hussein over allegations of land fraud. The suspect is accused of using forged court documents to cla...

item.title: Opposition Leader Calls for PM's Resignation Amid Protests and Police Crackdown
item.description: Rahul Gandhi, leader of India's main opposition party, has demanded Prime Minister Narendra Modi step down after police used force against student protesters demanding education reforms. The clash in ...

item.title: Kenya Police Raid Illegal Brewery Linked to Weapon Cache in Nakuru
item.description: Kenyan authorities uncovered an illegal brewery in Nakuru County that was producing unregulated ethanol and harboring weapons, including a pistol and ammunition. This discovery highlights the growing ...

item.title: Hamilton Police Charge 32 in Organized Home Depot Theft Ring
item.description: Hamilton police have charged 32 individuals in a coordinated series of thefts from multiple Home Depot stores. The investigation, dubbed 'Project Checkout,' was launched after a 25% rise in thefts was...

item.title: South Africa and World Bank Launch $1.5 Billion Loan to Boost Infrastructure Reforms
item.description: The South African government has secured a US$1.5 billion Development Policy Loan from the World Bank to advance infrastructure reforms across key sectors, including electricity, freight logistics, wa...

item.title: Hwang In-youp to Hold 'To You' Fan Meet in Manila
item.description: Hwang In-youp, the popular Korean actor known for his role in the drama 'To You', is set to return to Manila for a special fan meet. The event, scheduled for July 21, 2026, will provide fans with an o...

item.title: Deaf actor Kaylee Hottle, 18, dies in car accident after starring in Godzilla vs Kong
item.description: Kaylee Hottle, a deaf actor best known for her role as Jia in the 2021 film Godzilla vs Kong, has died following a car accident at the age of 18. Her father, Joshua Hottle, confirmed the tragic news v...

item.title: Perth paediatrician-turned-ISIS fighter reportedly imprisoned in Iraq after decade-long disappearance
item.description: Tareq Kamleh, a former Perth paediatrician who joined the Islamic State (IS) and became known as 'Dr Jihad,' is allegedly still alive and being held in an Iraqi jail nearly a decade after his death wa...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Completes Degree While Pregnant, Celebrates with Daughter
item.description: A Kenyan woman, Mwende, has graduated with her newborn daughter, Imani, by her side, marking a significant milestone in her life. The graduate shared photos from her graduation ceremony, highlighting ...

item.title: Safal Kiswahili Prize Announces Shortlisted Works for 10th Anniversary Celebration
item.description: The Safal Kiswahili Prize for African Literature has shortlisted seven manuscripts across fiction, poetry, and short story categories for its 10th anniversary celebration on July 24 in Dar es Salaam. ...

item.title: IIT Roorkee Clarifies Internal Advisory on Political Discussions Amid CJP Protests
item.description: The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee issued an internal advisory warning students and staff against participating in political discussions or activities without prior permission, which has...
